_note: Edit freely. This drives what the dashboard treats as relevant. Nothing here is locked in — it was drafted from the examples in your concept doc, not confirmed with you directly.

location.note: Set 2026-07-07 from Anthony's feedback (assumed 'Shanghai' in dictated message meant Chiang Mai — confirm if wrong).

watch_topics: Thai business news with tourism/hospitality relevance
Chiang Mai infrastructure: airport upgrades/expansion, road projects, major subsidies, government initiatives affecting tourism

intelligence_topics: AI capability breakthroughs (new models, research results, hardware) — concrete advances, not policy process
longevity & anti-aging: new research, findings, treatment/product/service trends

company_watchlist._note: Added 2026-07-04. 'Company News' rail panel — innovation announcements, breakthroughs, new products/capabilities ONLY. Explicitly NOT: earnings reports, stock moves, executive changes, routine corporate/PR news.

startup_radar._note: Added 2026-07-04. 'Startup Radar' rail panel — new startups, launches, funding rounds, and novel business models. Deliberately loose scope: the test is 'huh, that's a clever business' — general interest in new business ideas, not just investable ones.
spaces: any genuinely novel business model worth knowing about
new drinks related to coffee or chocolate

deprioritize: domestic politics commentary not tied to policy change
governance/diplomacy process stories (summits, dialogues, talks) with no binding outcome and no direct impact on Thailand tourism or my interests

learned_preferences.feedback: Very little Thai news beyond weather alerts and some TAT items. Keep checking Thai outlets; specifically watch Chiang Mai infrastructure (airport upgrades, tourism, major subsidies, government initiatives, road infrastructure) — direct business impact. No separate local-news card for now; still tuning.
learned_preferences.rule: Every run, explicitly query Thai outlets (Bangkok Post, The Nation, Thai PBS, Chiang Mai CityNews, TAT newsroom) for Thai business news and Chiang Mai infrastructure/policy developments. Route findings into existing sections (Opportunities for demand/infrastructure upside, Alerts for disruption). Absence of news is acceptable — never pad — but the searches must happen. Do NOT add a dedicated local-news card unless Anthony asks again.

learned_preferences.feedback: LESS: 'UN Global Dialogue on AI Governance' — totally irrelevant, no direct impact, no action for me. Would much rather see an AI breakthrough.
learned_preferences.rule: Exclude AI governance/policy meeting coverage unless it produces a binding decision that directly affects Anthony's business or tech he uses. Fill Intelligence slots with concrete capability/research breakthroughs instead.

source_preferences._note: Added 2026-07-03, clarified same day: 'news before it's news' is ADDITIVE, not exclusive. Major mainstream stories that genuinely matter still belong in the briefing — the goal is to ALSO stay ahead of public news, not to ignore it.
also_scan_early_sources: primary research: papers, preprints (arXiv, bioRxiv), university/lab press releases
niche newsletters, substacks, and expert blogs
YouTube talks/interviews with researchers, founders, operators
prediction markets and other early quantitative signals
regional/local sources for Thailand stories (often ahead of international coverage)
avoid: re-briefing a mainstream story that has been running unchanged for days (staleness is the problem, not the outlet)
stories whose only novelty is that another outlet repeated them
source_preferences.balance: A big mainstream story with real relevance always earns its slot. Early-signal stories compete for the remaining slots — aim for a visible share of the briefing (especially Hidden Gems and tier-2) coming from pre-mainstream sources.
source_preferences.exception: Alerts and Thai policy/visa stories should still use official and established sources (TMD, TAT, Royal Gazette, major Thai outlets) — accuracy beats earliness when the story is operational.

prediction_markets._note: Added 2026-07-04. Two uses: (1) INLINE — when a briefed story has a live Polymarket market, show its probability as a chip next to the confidence chip ('Market: 68% ▲3'); (2) 'Market Signals' rail panel — only markets that MOVED meaningfully (±5pts/week or crossed 50%) in Anthony's topic areas. Never a static odds list. Data constraint: Polymarket API and site are unreachable from the pipeline — get odds via web search (news coverage citing Polymarket, cached snippets); always state the as-of date; if a figure can't be verified with a dated source, skip it rather than guess. Label as 'crowd forecast', never as fact.
topics: AI (major model releases, AGI timelines, company milestones)
macro affecting travel/tourism (Fed cuts, recession odds, oil prices)

podcasts._note: Podcast Digest section: summarize new episodes only, with an 'underlying idea', a relevance line only when genuine, a listen/skim verdict, and a source-confidence label (transcript vs show notes).
shows: The Diary of a CEO (Steven Bartlett)
My First Million (Sam Parr & Shaan Puri)

alert_topics: major regulatory or visa changes affecting tourism
